_vars.raw_problem: Our company is experiencing performance issues with a sorting algorithm used in our data analysis application. I need you to analyze the current implementation and optimize it. Please ensure that the output retains the correct order of elements after sorting and can handle large datasets efficiently. You should also provide a comparison with at least one alternative algorithm to demonstrate the improvements. Be prepared to present your findings in a report, including any assumptions made during your analysis. Time is of the essence, as we need this resolved in the next two weeks.
_vars.student_framing: [{"section_type": "Problem Definition", "content": "The problem is about a sorting algorithm that is not performing well in our data analysis application. We need to find out why it is slow and how to make it faster."}, {"section_type": "Objectives", "content": "1. Analyze the current sorting algorithm. 2. Optimize the algorithm to improve performance. 3. Ensure that the sorting keeps elements in the correct order. 4. Compare it with another sorting algorithm."}, {"section_type": "Data Requirements", "content": "We need to check how the current algorithm works with large datasets and what kind of data we are sorting, like numbers or strings."}, {"section_type": "Assumptions", "content": "I assume the current sorting algorithm is a common one. I also assume that the data we are sorting is not too complicated."}, {"section_type": "Timeline", "content": "We have two weeks to complete this task, so we need to plan our time well to analyze, optimize, and compare the algorithms."}, {"section_type": "Report Preparation", "content": "The report should include the analysis of the current algorithm, the optimized version, the comparison with another algorithm, and any assumptions made."}]

criteria.name: Problem Identification
criteria.description: Clearly identifies and articulates the key components of the ill-defined problem, including input and output specifications.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately describes the problem with all necessary components and constraints, using appropriate terminology.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify crucial components of the problem or misinterprets the problem requirements.

criteria.name: Task Specification
criteria.description: Translates the ill-defined problem into a well-defined task with clear objectives and steps to solve it.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Defines a comprehensive and clear task that outlines a logical approach to solving the problem.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides an ambiguous or incomplete task definition that lacks clarity or logical structure.

criteria.name: Algorithm Selection Consideration
criteria.description: Considers appropriate algorithms for sorting or searching based on the problem context and requirements.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Selects an optimal algorithm that is well-suited for the specific problem characteristics and justifies the choice effectively.
criteria.poor_indicator: Chooses a suboptimal or irrelevant algorithm without proper justification.

typical_errors: Misunderstanding the problem requirements leading to incorrect task framing.
Neglecting to specify input and output clearly, resulting in ambiguity.
Selecting an inappropriate algorithm based on the problem context.

best_framing.base: Our company is experiencing performance issues with a sorting algorithm used in our data analysis application. I need you to analyze the current implementation and optimize it. Please ensure that the output retains the correct order of elements after sorting and can handle large datasets efficiently. You should also provide a comparison with at least one alternative algorithm to demonstrate the improvements. Be prepared to present your findings in a report, including any assumptions made during your analysis. Time is of the essence, as we need this resolved in the next two weeks.

entries.title: Current Algorithm
entries.description: Assume the current sorting algorithm is a specific implementation (e.g., QuickSort, MergeSort) that needs to be identified for effective analysis.
entries.addresses_gap: What is the current sorting algorithm being used?

entries.title: Performance Issues
entries.description: Assume that the performance issues may include factors such as time complexity, space complexity, or stability of sorting, which need to be specified.
entries.addresses_gap: What specific performance issues are we encountering?

entries.title: Performance Metrics
entries.description: Clarify which performance metrics (e.g., execution time, memory usage, scalability) should be used to evaluate the sorting algorithm's efficiency.
entries.addresses_gap: What criteria should be used to evaluate the efficiency of the algorithms?

entries.title: Output Verification
entries.description: The solution should ensure that the sorted output retains the correct order of elements and can handle datasets of specified sizes (e.g., up to 1 million elements).
entries.addresses_gap: Ensures the output retains the correct order of elements after sorting.

entries.title: Algorithm Comparison
entries.description: Success will also be measured by the effectiveness of the comparison with at least one alternative sorting algorithm, demonstrating clear improvements in performance.
entries.addresses_gap: Provide a comparison with at least one alternative algorithm to demonstrate the improvements.

entries.title: In Scope
entries.description: The analysis will focus on optimizing the current sorting algorithm and comparing it with one alternative algorithm.
entries.addresses_gap: Defines the specific focus of the task.

entries.title: Out of Scope
entries.description: The task will not include modifications to the data analysis application beyond the sorting algorithm.
entries.addresses_gap: Clarifies what the analysis will not cover.

best_framing.coverage_notes: This framing addresses all rubric criteria by clearly identifying the problem components, specifying the task with logical steps, and considering appropriate algorithms for selection based on the problem context.

criticism: Your framing identifies the problem as a sorting algorithm that is slow, but it lacks specificity regarding the performance issues and the current algorithm being used. For example, stating that 'the current sorting algorithm is a common one' does not provide enough detail to understand the context. Additionally, while you mention optimizing the algorithm, you do not specify what metrics will be used to measure performance improvements, which is crucial for a clear task specification.
better_alternative: A stronger framing would include specific details about the current sorting algorithm, such as its name and known performance issues (e.g., time complexity or stability). You should also clarify the performance metrics that will be used to evaluate the algorithm's efficiency and ensure that the output retains the correct order of elements after sorting. Including a section on the scope of the task, such as what is in and out of scope, would also help to define the task more clearly.

per_criterion_scores.internal_note: The problem is vaguely defined without specific details about the algorithm or performance issues.
The objectives are outlined, but lack clarity on performance metrics and specific steps for optimization.
No specific algorithms are mentioned, and there is no justification for the choice of algorithms.

strengths: You clearly outline the objectives for analyzing and optimizing the sorting algorithm.
The timeline is well-defined, indicating urgency for completing the task.
refinements_correctly_selected: Optimize the algorithm to improve performance.
Ensure that the sorting keeps elements in the correct order.
Compare it with another sorting algorithm.
refinements_missed: Specify the current sorting algorithm being used.
Define what is in and out of scope for the analysis.
